title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
description: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

title: Anthropic is having a month
description: A human really borks things at Anthropic for the second time this week.

title: Salesforce announces an AI-heavy makeover for Slack, with 30 new features
description: Slack just got a whole lot more useful.

title: OpenAI, not yet public, raises $3B from retail investors in monster $122B fund raise
description: OpenAI's latest funding round, led by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, values the AI lab at $852 billion as it nears an IPO.

title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
description: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
description: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

title: Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

title: Exclusive: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early-stage AI startups
description: Runway is launching a $10 million fund and startup program to back companies building with its AI video models, as it pushes toward interactive, real-time “video intelligence” applications.

title: With its new app store, Ring bets on AI to go beyond home security
description: Ring's app store will allow the company to target broader use cases beyond security, like elder care or business needs.

title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
description: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
description: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
description: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll.

title: Mantis Biotech is making ‘digital twins’ of humans to help solve medicine’s data availability problem
description: Mantis takes disparate sources of data to make synthetic datasets that can be used to build so-called "digital twins" of the human body, representing anatomy, physiology and behavior.
